text.title: GraphRAG Knowledge Graph Construction
text.summary: How GraphRAG builds a structured knowledge graph from raw documents: entity extraction, relationship extraction, entity resolution, and community detection.

rule.statement: Use traditional NER (SpaCy, GliNER) for entity detection and LLMs only for relationship extraction — reduces indexing cost by ~60% with comparable entity recall.
rule.rationale: LLM-based entity extraction is the primary cost driver during indexing. NER models run locally and fast; relationships require semantic understanding that LLMs provide.

rule.statement: Define an explicit entity type ontology before extraction; constrain the LLM to that ontology in every extraction prompt.
rule.rationale: Unconstrained extraction produces thousands of unique entity types that defeat community detection and make graph traversal unpredictable.

example.note: Schema-guided extraction prompt — entity types explicitly listed, JSON output required
example.reference: Entity extraction prompt with schema constraints and gleaning

rule.statement: Run embedding-based entity resolution (cosine similarity > 0.9) before graph construction to merge duplicate nodes.
rule.rationale: Duplicate entities fragment communities and corrupt multi-hop reasoning. "OpenAI" and "Open AI" as separate nodes break relationship traversal.

antipattern.description: Skipping entity resolution and inserting raw extracted entities directly into the graph.
antipattern.reason: Duplicate nodes receive separate community assignments; queries against one node miss relationships attached to the duplicate.

section.title: Community Detection

rule.statement: Use the Leiden algorithm hierarchically with max_cluster_size=10 as the default starting point; tune resolution parameter for granularity.
rule.rationale: Microsoft GraphRAG uses Leiden for its proven convergence properties. Hierarchical detection produces multiple abstraction levels for global queries.

rule.statement: Cache community summaries at index time; regenerate only when community membership changes (delta indexing).
rule.rationale: Global search costs 2000+ tokens per community summary. Regenerating all summaries on each query exhausts token budgets in 3-5 iterations of a ReAct loop.

section.p: Community levels: Level 0 = fine-grained (original graph), Level 1 = aggregated themes, Level 2+ = higher abstractions, Root = entire corpus summary.

section.title: Technology Stack

list.item: Microsoft GraphRAG — full pipeline with community detection; best for batch indexing
Neo4j + neo4j-graphrag-python — production-ready hybrid (vector + graph) search
LlamaIndex PropertyGraph — flexible extraction; multiple extractor support
LightRAG — 10x token reduction; trades relationship granularity for cost
NetworkX — in-memory prototyping; no ops overhead

section.reference: Microsoft GraphRAG settings.yaml with key parameters
Generic and domain-specific entity/relationship schema definitions
